Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-15 Agenda item 2: Interim report for the first half of 2026, including a year-end forecast for the municipality and its municipal companies. Reference number: 26KS25 Distributed to: All committees AB Gavlegårdarna Gävle Energi AB Gävle Hamn AB Gavlefastigheter i Gävle AB Gävle Parkeringsservice AB SG finance department Decision The Municipal Council decides, in accordance with the proposal from the Municipal Board, to: Approve the Building and Development Committee's deficit, as the costs are considered to be one-time in nature, referring to the municipality's strong financial position. Instruct the Municipal Board to return to the Municipal Council with proposals for handling the deficits of the other committees, in conjunction with the second interim report. Assign the Municipal Board to focus on and develop the municipal group's work on its own emissions in order to achieve the Municipal Council's goal of climate neutrality. Otherwise, to adopt and approve the submitted interim report for the first half of 2026, including the year-end forecast for the municipality and its municipal companies.

Description of the matter The Governance and Support sector has compiled the interim report 1 for 2026 for the municipality and its municipal companies. The interim report presents the financial results and forecasts of the committees, the municipality, and the wholly-owned companies, as well as investments and forecasts for the full year. Performance monitoring is carried out at the committee and company level. Page 9 (80)

Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-15 A comprehensive report, along with a description of activities and goal achievement at the municipal and municipal group level, will be reported in the second quarterly report. For the period of January-April 2026, the municipality of Gävle shows a financial result that is affected by increased costs in areas such as social care, the Act on Support and Services for Persons with Disabilities (LSS), placements, and winter road maintenance. At the same time, higher tax revenues, net financial income, and certain operational surpluses contribute positively. The full-year forecast for the municipality is 194.6 million SEK, which represents a positive result but lower than the budget. Several committees are forecasting deficits by the end of the year. The largest negative deviations are expected to occur in:

  • The Municipal Planning Committee (-33.7 million SEK)
  • The Social Services Committee (-30.0 million SEK)
  • The Employment and Disability Committee (-20.0 million SEK)
  • The Education Committee (-10.0 million SEK).

The population in Gävle has increased slightly during the first few months of the year, primarily due to positive net immigration. At the same time, the number of children and young people is decreasing, while the number of elderly people is increasing, especially in the group over 80 years old. The target for a strong financial position is projected to be 53.4 percent, which exceeds the target of 50 percent. The municipality's balance sheet result is projected to be 187.3 million SEK, and the municipality has no prior deficits to recover. Overall, despite some projected deficits in operations, the municipality of Gävle shows a strong financial position. The municipal group is forecasting a positive result for the full year, and the municipal companies are consistently reporting positive result forecasts. The investment rate remains high, even though several major projects have been postponed. Page 10 (80)

Motions: Åsa Wiklund-Lång (S), Daniel Olsson (S), Kristofer Dahlgren (L) and Jörgen Edsvik (S) propose that the proposal from the municipal board be approved.

Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-15 Agenda item: § 3 Investment and development budget 2027–2030 Reference number: 26KS24 To be sent to: Municipal committees Gavlefastigheter Gävle kommun AB AB Gavlegårdarna Gävle Vatten AB Gävle Energi AB Gävle Hamn AB Gävle Parkeringsservice AB SG ekonomi Decision The Municipal Council decides, in accordance with the proposal from the Municipal Board, to:

  • Approve the proposed investment budget for 2027, with a plan for 2028–2030 (Appendix 2), including investments in municipal companies that are directed towards the municipality's tax-funded operations.
  • Approve the proposed development budget (Appendix 3) for the municipality.
  • Based on the proposed development budget, approve a budgeted development result of 87.2 million SEK for 2027, including direct costs.
  • Approve the proposed investment frameworks (Appendix 2) for commercial investments related to the companies Gavlefastigheter AB, AB Gavlegårdarna, Gävle Energi AB, Gävle Parkeringsservice AB, Gävle Hamn AB, and Gävle Vatten AB, and that the operational decision-making process should apply to the implementation of investments within the municipal group.
  • Under the Municipal Board, allocate 80 million SEK for operational premises, divided into 40 million SEK for 2027 and 40 million SEK for 2028. Operational premises include activities currently managed by the Urban Planning Committee and the Municipal Board.
  • Instruct the Culture and Leisure Committee to prepare a proposal for an investment in storage facilities at Kastvallen. This proposal will be presented to the Municipal Board in September.

Description of the matter The municipal plan with the annual budget for 2027 and the planning period 2028–2030 will be decided by the Municipal Council in November, as planned. To ensure adequate lead time for planning investment activities, the exploitation and investment budget for 2027, with a planning period for 2028–2030, will be decided by the Municipal Council in June. The documentation for the investment budget was presented to the Finance Committee in March and April. The documentation outlines the investment needs per committee for the years 2026–2030, according to the committee structure that will be in effect from January 1, 2027. Appendix 2 shows a breakdown of the investments for 2026 as well, based on the committee structure that will be in effect in 2027. However, Appendix 2 does not represent a change to the investment budget within the committee structure for 2026 for those committees affected by the new committee structure at the beginning of the year, as the decision pertains to the years 2027–2030. Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-15 The development and investment activities in Gävle municipality have a comprehensive investment accounting system. For development projects, this also includes operational accounting and revenue accounting. The development activities aim to be self-financing, which means that revenues should exceed expenses. Each development project should be tracked with a development net figure. A positive development net figure corresponds to a financing rate of over 100 percent. A development net figure can be negative if this has been decided in the project's original total budget. As preparation for upcoming investment decisions and to enable strategic real estate decisions, the relevant committees will be tasked with investigating the conditions, in connection with the regular investment decisions for 2027-2030. Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-15 Agenda item 4: Environmental Report for Gävle Municipality for 2025 Case number: 26KS5 To be sent to: All committees and boards. Decision The Municipal Council decides, in accordance with the proposal from the Municipal Board, to approve the environmental report for Gävle Municipality for the year 2025. Description of the matter Gävle Municipality's environmental strategy is reviewed annually through an environmental report. The environmental report is used to visualize the progress of environmental and climate work and serves as a basis for continued planning and implementation of the strategy. The environmental report includes a review of 34 goals and 60 indicators, with the circular economy plan integrated into the review. Statistics for the indicators have been collected from designated contact persons within the municipal organization and from national monitoring systems. During the year, the activities within the municipal organization have reported on completed measures. The goal analyses have been prepared in collaboration with relevant representatives within the municipal organization. On December 15, 2025, the Municipal Council decided to update the environmental strategy. This report reviews the environmental strategy before the update. The environmental report compares the 2025 goal status with the year 2020, when the strategy was revised. The purpose is to show how development has progressed over time. The development since 2020 shows that the environmental work is moving in the right direction. Fewer red goals and an increase in both green and yellow goals indicate that the measures taken are having an effect. Compared to 2020, the percentage of green goals has increased from 9 percent to 18 percent, while the percentage of yellow goals has increased from 47 percent to 59 percent, and the percentage of red goals has decreased from 44 percent to 23 percent. Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-15 Agenda item 5: Annual report and discharge of liability for Gästrike Återvinnare 2025 Case number: 26KS18 To be sent to: Gästrike Återvinnare Decision The Municipal Council decides, in accordance with the proposal from the Municipal Board, to grant discharge of liability to the board of directors and to each individual member of the board for the fiscal year 2025.

Description of the matter Gästrike Återvinnare is a municipal association with a board of directors. In accordance with Chapter 9, Section 13 of the Municipal Act, the auditors must submit an audit report to the municipal council of each member association. This also means that the municipal council must, by the end of June, decide whether or not to grant discharge of liability.

The auditors for Gästrike Återvinnare have assessed that the board of directors for Gästrike Återvinnare:

  • has conducted the operations in a suitable and economically satisfactory manner,
  • that the accounts are, in all material respects, fair,
  • that the internal controls have been sufficient,
  • that the result, according to the annual report, is consistent with the financial and operational goals set by the municipal council for sound financial management.

The auditors recommend that each municipal council grant discharge of liability to the board of directors and to each individual member of the board.

Comments on the matter Per-Åke Fredriksson (L) Page 20 (80)

According to Section 1 of the law granting municipalities the right to charge fees for certain use of public spaces, etc., if a permit according to Section 3, Paragraph 1 of the Public Order Act has been granted for a public space under the municipality's administration, the municipality may charge for the use of that space. The right to charge also applies to the use of a public square or similar space that the municipality has made available for general sales. On June 19, 2023, in item 84, the municipal council decided to adopt a fee schedule for the use of public spaces within the municipality of Gävle. The Department of Living Environment has therefore considered it appropriate to review the general provisions and fees in the fee schedule for the use of public spaces. One purpose of the review has been to create a clear structure that simplifies implementation. Another intention of the review has been to introduce more nuanced fees for market trading in the fee schedule. The Department of Living Environment has also reviewed whether the fees are reasonable, including by comparing them with reference municipalities: Örebro, Uppsala, Västerås, Norrköping, Linköping, and Eskilstuna, which are comparable to the municipality of Gävle.

One of the most significant changes in the new fee schedule is the proposal to allow outdoor seating areas to operate year-round. New categories are being introduced for rental businesses related to outdoor equipment and activities, digital signage, and the sale of organically grown products. A new category for other uses is being introduced, intended for cases where the purpose of the use cannot be classified under another category. Fees for evening and night shifts on public squares are being introduced, and the so-called "Stockholm pricing" is also being implemented, which allows for differentiated fees within construction projects.

Another change is that several categories are being removed or merged. Previously, there were many different sales categories; several of these are being combined to simplify fee collection and provide better predictability for permit holders. Market trading spaces are being simplified to refer to the number of days the space is used, providing flexibility in how the permit is formulated. Page 28(80)

Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-15 The area known as "Bondtorget," located within Stortorget where certain sales activities have previously been exempt from fees, will be removed. The purpose is to clarify that it is the intended use of the space that determines whether it is subject to fees, not the specific area being used. Another change is the introduction of lower fees. This primarily applies to outdoor seating areas, events, and advertising signs. "Urban gardening" is proposed to be made exempt from fees. The municipality's own fee for processing permits for the use of public spaces will be eliminated. According to Chapter 2, Section 5 of the Municipal Act, a municipality can only charge fees for services and amenities that it is obligated to provide if it is required by law or other regulations. The Act on the municipality's right to charge fees for certain uses of public spaces, etc., states that the municipality can charge for the permit holder's use of the public space, not for the municipality's processing of cases as defined in Chapter 3, Section 1 of the Public Order Act. The zoning structure within the fee schedule for the use of public spaces has also been revised. Zone A has been expanded to include not only Stortorget but also Rådhustorget, Söderbacken, and Södra Kungsgatan up to Södermalmstorg, as well as nearby areas to Stortorget and Rådhustorget, and a portion of Drottninggatan. Zone B has also been expanded to include Boulognerskogen and parts of Brynäs, including Ahlgrens torg. It is proposed that event fees be differentiated based on the zones. Previously, only the intended use of the space could determine the fee. The Municipal Planning Committee is proposed to be delegated the authority by the Municipal Council to decide on annual index adjustments to the fee schedule. The fees (base fees) will be based on the consumer price index (total index with 2020 as the base year) for the month of October (the base month) of the year preceding the calendar year in which the fees take effect. For each subsequent calendar year, the Municipal Planning Committee may decide to adjust the fees on January 1st to reflect index changes up to the October month closest to the beginning of the current calendar year. The comparison should always be made with the previous October month. The outgoing fee should never be lower than the fee from the previous year. For the fee schedule that the Municipal Council decides to implement starting July 1, 2026, the base month is October 2025 (total index with 2020 as the base year) and will remain in effect until the Municipal Planning Committee decides to make index adjustments according to the authority delegated by the Municipal Council. Revision of governing documents related to pensions Reference number: 26KS257 To be sent to: HR Director, Financial Director, HR strateg/manager in each sector, HR strategist or equivalent in GSAB companies, Gästrike Rescue Service, Gästrike Recycling, Municipal Secretary.

Decision The Municipal Council decides, in accordance with the proposal from the Municipal Board, to:

  • With effect from 2027-01-01, abolish the Pension Policy, Guidelines for OPF-KL and PBL, and the Guidelines for pension issues in the Municipality of Gävle.
  • With effect from 2027-01-01, introduce the Guidelines - Pension for employees in the Municipality of Gävle, and the Pension Regulations for elected officials in the Municipality of Gävle.
  • Urge the GSAB companies to adjust the remuneration of elected officials from 2027-01-01.

Description of the issue Currently, there is a pension policy that covers both employees and elected officials, as well as separate guidelines for elected officials and employees. The proposed change is to have one document for employees and one for elected officials. In addition to separating employees and elected officials, the proposal aims to significantly reduce the amount of text to make it more easily understandable.

The current governing documents primarily consist of text taken from previous OPF-KL, which leads to some ambiguity without actually adding any real value. Furthermore, there are some provisions that conflict with the central agreements and OPF-KR, mainly related to the increase in the retirement age.

In practice, the proposed change will only add a statement regarding the Municipality of Gävle's view on the application of partial reduction benefits for senior employees. It will also provide clarifications on how decisions regarding special contractual pensions are made, and it will make it possible to update the regulations to align with the current OPF without requiring a new political decision. Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-15 Agenda item 16: Motion from the Left Party (V) - Secure Housing Case number: 24KS161 To be sent to: The Care and Welfare Committee Decision The Municipal Council decides, in accordance with the proposal from the Municipal Board, to consider the motion answered.

Description of the matter At the Municipal Council meeting on 2024-03-25, agenda item 17, Owe Hellberg (V) presented the motion, titled as above. A statement regarding the motion has been received from the Care and Welfare Committee.

The motion highlights that the number of elderly people will increase significantly in the coming years, and that the municipality should build secure housing facilities to reduce the pressure on existing care homes. It is true that the number of people over 65 will increase in the future, but this does not necessarily mean that more care homes or apartments are needed. This development reflects that the population is generally staying healthier for longer, and that there is a trend towards people staying in their own homes, with support from services such as home care. The municipality of Gävle currently, and in the foreseeable future, does not have a need for additional care and welfare apartments, and there are available apartments for people who have been approved for care home placement.

We are positive about the idea of more secure housing facilities. By building them, we can provide a sense of security for the elderly, allowing them to live in a facility tailored to their needs without having to leave their neighborhood. Secure housing facilities also enable us to combat involuntary loneliness among the elderly. Gavlegårdarna has been tasked with investigating the possibility of building a secure housing facility in Bergby. However, given current legislation and high construction costs, the project has been assessed as difficult to complete. Therefore, efforts are being made to explore alternative solutions to find the best possible way forward. Page 44 (80)

The proposer requests that Sportoteket be reorganized into a "Leisure Bank" and that the current activities be highlighted more clearly on the municipality's website and on social media. The proposer describes the advantages of the "Leisure Bank" concept, such as their website with current inventory status and a list of items, as well as the support from a main organization and partners.

The proposer emphasizes that Sportoteket is one of the most important initiatives that Gävle municipality has undertaken to enable more children and young people to participate in sports. Sportoteket is successful in many ways.

In the statement from the Culture and Leisure Committee, it is stated that the investigation proposed by the proposer was already initiated in 2023. It is well known what the conditions, advantages, and disadvantages are of transforming Sportoteket into a "Leisure Bank." No further investigation is necessary at this time.

However, the committee initiated an expanded communication effort as early as 2023, which means that the lessons learned about how to reach the target group for Sportoteket are well known. Page 46 (80)

Municipal Council Meeting date: 2026-06-15 Agenda item 18: Motion from the Left Party (V) - Neighborhood Networks Case number: 24KS539 To be sent to: Governance and Support, Unit for Social Sustainability Decision The Municipal Council decides, in accordance with the proposal from the Municipal Board, to reject the motion.

Description of the matter At the Municipal Council meeting on December 16, 2024, agenda item 34, Owe Hellberg (V) presented the motion titled above. The proposer requests that the municipality restart the neighborhood networks for the districts of Öster and Nordost. While the ambition is commendable, we do not believe that the conditions for implementing it currently exist.

Neighborhood networks create opportunities for collaboration and information exchange between municipal departments, authorities, associations, and citizens. For the network to function, it requires citizens and associations to drive ideas and proposals based on what they believe the district needs. In Nordost and Öster, there is no primary school, and there have not been enough associations participating for the network to function as intended. For the neighborhood network to be successful, an active association life is needed, but the level of activity in the associations has previously been too low to enable the dialogue we desire. When and if there are better conditions, including a more active association life, to start a network, we will work to make it happen. What is most important to us is the result, not the form of how we achieve it. Currently, our priority is to find other ways to reach citizens. With that said, we view it positively if we have more active associations that can address issues that are important in the district, and in that case, it will become relevant to restart the neighborhood network.

tldr: The municipality of Gävle held a meeting where they made decisions regarding the budget for future construction projects and investments, and approved reports on environmental and economic matters. They also discussed parking spaces for motorhomes and nature reserves.
lattlast:
In the municipality of Gävle, the politicians in the municipal council have reviewed important plans for the coming years. They have approved a budget for investments and land development between the years 2027 and 2030. This means that a plan has been established for how funds will be used for future construction projects and the development of land within the municipality.

The politicians also reviewed financial reports. They examined the performance of the municipality and its companies during the first half of 2026. They also approved the annual reports for Gästrike Räddningstjänst, which provides fire services, and Gästrike Återvinnare, which handles recycling.

Several issues related to the environment and leisure activities were also on the agenda. These included the Almkärret nature reserve and proposals to create parking spaces for motorhomes at the outdoor area in Hemlingby. Discussions also took place regarding regulations for the use of public spaces, such as sidewalks and squares, including a review of associated fees.

The meeting also included proposals from various parties, known as motions. These proposals included plans to invest more in outdoor activities and to build assisted living facilities for the elderly. Work was also carried out to update the regulations regarding the management of pensions in the municipality's governing documents.
